title: Effective polyclonal antibodies against the virulence-associated protein D (vapD) of Helicobacter pylori, obtained from recombinant VapD
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"bj8g","text":"Helicobacter pylori is a microorganism associated with serious gastric pathologies. This bacterium presents specific genes that encode for different virulence factors associated with the development of gastric disease. The VapD protein has rarely been studied, although it has been previously demonstrated its participation in the protection of Helicobacter pylori within gastric cells. In the present work, we document the protocols developed to generate the VapD recombinant protein and the subsequent production of polyclonal antibodies. Our research group faced several problems throughout the trials; however, all of them were successfully solved. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":0,"length":19},{"style":"italic","offset":345,"length":19}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: The vapD gene was amplified by PCR. The PCR mixture was prepared as follows: %s %s

Program the PCR thermal cycler with the following conditions: denaturation at 94 °C for 00:05:00 , followed by 30 cycles of denaturation at 94 °C for 00:01:00 , annealing at 52 °C for 00:01:00 and extension at 72 °C for 00:01:00 ; and a final extension at 72 °C for 00:10:00 .
Run the PCR product by electrophoresis on agarose gel and visualize it on an UV transilluminator.
Purify the PCR product from agarose gel electrophoresis using the MinElute Gel Extraction Kit following the manufacturer's instructions.
Quantify the PCR product by measuring its absorbance at 260/280 nm using a spectrophotometer.
Generate the VapD-6xHis fusion vector using the aLICator™ LIC Cloning and Expression System Kit 3:

Transform by electroporation 1 µL - 6 µL of the annealing reaction into 50 µL of competent E. coli DH5α cells. Deliver the electric pulse under the following conditions: 1.8 kV, 200 mA for 0.5 milisecs. If an electroporator is unavailable, thermal shock can be used for transformation.

Spread 50 µL of the cells on an LB agar plate containing ampicillin ( 100 µg, /mL ). If no colonies appear after transformation, centrifuge the bacterial culture at 500 xg, discard the supernatant, and add 100 µl of fresh medium. Resuspend the cell pellet thoroughly and spread it onto a plate.
Add 2 µL of 5× LIC buffer, 1 µL of T4 DNA polymerase, 20 ng of purified PCR product, and enough water to adjust the final volume to 9 µL . Vortex briefly.
Incubate the reaction mixture at Room temperature for 00:05:00 .
Stop the reaction by adding 0.6 µL of 0.5 Molarity (M) EDTA and mix well.
For annealing, add 1 µL of pLATE31 LIC-ready vector ( 60 ng ) to the reaction. Vortex briefly.

Incubate the annealing reaction at Room temperature for 00:05:00 . We attempted to clone vapD gene into various expression vectors, including pGEx-4T-2, pET-28b(+), and pET30-GBFusion1, but were not successful.
Pick, at least 10 colonies and perform colony PCR screening using the same conditions to amplify the vapD gene (see steps 1 and 2).
Inoculate 5 mL of LB medium containing ampicillin ( 100 µg, /mL ) with the positive colonies.
Incubate the tubes at 37 °C, Overnight in a shaking incubator at 200 rpm, °C, , /min .
Extract the plasmid from saturated cultures using the PureDirex Plasmid miniPREP Kit, following the manufacturer’s instructions.
Run the plasmids by electrophoresis on agarose gel.

Screened the positives plasmids for the vapD gene by Sanger sequencing to ensure that there are not PCR-introduced mutations, using the LIC forward and LIC reverse sequencing primers included in the aLICator™ Ligation Independent Cloning and Expression System Kit 3.

Transform by electroporation competent E. coli Rosetta (DE3) cells with 10 ng of the expression vector (pLATE31-VapD-6xHis). Deliver the electric pulse under the following conditions: 1.8 kV, 200 mA for 0.5milisecs. If an electroporator is unavailable, thermal shock can be used for transformation. We attempted to clone vapD gene into BL21 (DE3), but the attempted was unsuccessful.
Incubate the plates at 37 °C, Overnight .

Inoculate a 15 ml tube with a screw tap with 3 mL of LB medium containing ampicillin( 100 µg, /mL ) with a single colony of E. coli Rosetta (DE3) containing the expression vector (pLATE31-VapD-6xHis).
Incubate the tube at 37 °C, Overnight in a shaking incubator at 200 rpm, °C, , /min .
Inoculate 30 mL of LB medium in a 250 ml Erlenmeyer flask with 0.5 mL of saturated overnight culture.

Transfer the culture to a 50 ml conical tube and centrifuge at 3000 rpm, 4 °C, , discard the supernatant. The cell pellet can be stored at -70 °C until use. We tested different concentrations of IPT (0.1, 0.5, and 1.0 mM) and incubation times, including overnight expression, but observed no differences. The optimal conditions were as previously mentioned. To verify the correct expression of the recombinant vapD protein (rVapD), take 20 µL of the culture before centrifugation, add 2.5 µL of 4× BMR buffer, and boil it for 00:03:00 . Separate the sample using SDS-PAGE and visualize it with Coomassie brilliant blue staining.
Grow the cells at 37 °C in a shaking incubator at 200 rpm, °C, , /min to mild-log phase (OD 600nm ca. 0.5).
Resuspend the cell pellet in 10 mL of lysis buffer supplemented with EDTA-free protease inhibitor cocktail and PMSF 0.2 millimolar (mM) .

Lyse the cells by sonication, pulses of 00:00:10 at 70% of amplitude with intervals of 00:00:10 between each pulse, up to reach a clear cell lysate (ca. 00:10:00 ). If a sonic dismembrator is unavailable, the cell lysis can be performed using lysozyme as follows: Add 1 mg, /mL of lysozyme and incubate for 01:00:00 at 4 °C with gentle agitation at end-over-end rotator. Add 0.7% sarkosyl, 3% Triton X-100, and 20 millimolar (mM) CHAPS, then mix gently for 00:15:00 at 4 °C using an end-over-end rotator.
Add the protein solution onto the column at a flow rate of 1 mL, /min .
Add an appropriate amount of Ni-NTA resin to a chromatography column. Once the column is packed, equilibrate it with two column volumes of equilibration buffer.

Centrifuge at 15000 x g, , 20 minutes and recover the supernatant in conical tubes of 50 ml. Sarkosyl can be used at higher concentrations (1-10%) to maximize the recovery of insoluble protein. However, we observed that the addition of sarkosyl (concentration >1%) increased the viscosity of the protein solution, with higher concentrations leading to greater viscosity. If the protein solution becomes too viscous, it can be passed through sterile gauze and gently squeezed, stopping when the filtered solution begins to thicken again.
Analyze the purified rVapD. Take 20 µL of the sample, add 2.5 µL of 4× BMR buffer, and boil it for 00:03:00 .
Elute the rVapD protein from the resin with five volumes of elution buffer at a flow rate of 1 mL, /min .

Pass the fractions containing the rVapD through a HiPrep 26/10 Desalting at a flow rate of 1 mL, /min to remove imidazole. If a desalting column is unavailable, the imidazole can be removed by dialysis.

A total of six six-week-old Balb/C mice were used. The mice were intraperitoneally immunized five times, fortnightly, with 10 µg of rVapD diluted in PBS 1× and mixed with Freund’s incomplete adjuvant, in a total volume of 200 µL per animal. Four serum samples were collected. After the final sample collection, the mice were euthanized with CO 2 . The experiment is depicted in Fig 1. Fig 1. Representation of the immunization schedule. Sera were collected for antibody analysis at specific time point: before the first inoculation (pre-immune serum) and at 15, 45, and 60 days after each booster doce (gray arrows). The final blood collection was performed on day 75 (red arrow). This figure was created with https://www.biorender.com/
Dilute rVapD in carbonate-bicarbonate buffer a final concentration of 100 ng, /mL .
Coat a flat-bottomed 96-well high binding plate with 100 µL, /well of rVapD diluted in carbonate buffer.

For the dot blot, fix a nitrocellulose membrane with different concentrations of rVapD Overnight at 4 °C . The initial concentration is 1 µg, /mL of rVapD, serially diluted two-fold down to 0.03 µg, /mL .
Stop the reaction with 100 µL, /well of 3 Molarity (M) sulfuric acid.
Add 100 µL, /well of the OPD developer solution.
Incubate the plate with the secondary antibody mouse antibody H+L coupled to horseradish peroxidase for 01:30:00 at 37 °C .
Incubate the plates for 02:00:00 at 37 °C with 100 µL, /well of serum from immunized mice, prepared in serial two-fold dilutions ranging from 1:500 to 1:32,000.
Wash three times 200 µL, /well of PBT.
Wash the membrane three times with PBT.
Wash the membrane three times with PBT.
Block the membranes with 5% skimmed milk diluted in PBT and incubate for 00:40:00 with gentle shaking at Room temperature .
Visualize the enzymatic reaction with the DAB developer solution and stop it with distilled water.
Load in 20% SDS-PAGE gels with different concentrations of rVapD, and run.
Transfer the SDS-PAGE gel to a PVDF membrane for 01:00:00 at a constant voltage of 100 V at 4 °C .
Block the unoccupied sites with 3% skimmed milk in PBS 1× for 01:00:00 at Room temperature with gentle rotation.
Incubate the membrane with the secondary antibody (anti-mouse antibody H+L coupled to horseradish peroxidase) at a 1:2,000 dilution for 01:00:00 at 4 °C with gentle agitation.
Incubate the membrane with mouse serum (anti-VapD) as the primary antibody in a dilution of 1:2,000 Overnight at 4 °C with gentle agitation.
Wash three times with PBT for 00:10:00 .

Grow Human gastric adenocarcinoma cell lines (AGS) in DMEM supplemented with 5% fetal bovine serum, 100 IU/mL penicillin, 100 mg, /mL streptomycin, 0.2% NaHCO3, 10 millimolar (mM) HEPES, and 2 millimolar (mM) glutamine.
Wash three times with PBS 1×.
Remove the supernatant, and add 2.5 mL of DMEM containing 200 µg, /mL gentamicin.
Wash three times with PBS 1× and inoculate H. pylori strain 26695 at MOI of 100 (multiplicity of infection of 100 bacteria/cell) in 2.5 mL of DMEM.
Seed 2.5x10 5 AGS cells in 35 mm Petri dishes.
Grow H. pylori strain 26695 in blood agar plates supplemented with 5% fetal bovine serum.
Wash the cells with PBS 1× .
Wash the cells with PBS 1×.
Wash the cells with PBS 1× and permeabilize them with permeabilizing solution for 00:05:00 at 4 °C .
Wash the cells with PBS 1×.
Place three coverslips in each Petri dish and incubate at 37 °C in a humidified atmosphere in the presence of 5% CO 2 for up to 48:00:00 .
Observe the samples under an epifluorescence microscope.
Wash the protein A/G agarose beads ( 20 µL per reaction) two times with 200 µL of lysis buffer supplemented with EDTA-free protease inhibitor cocktail and 0.2 millimolar (mM) PMSF.
Incubate the beads with 5 µL of mouse serum (anti-VapD) diluted in lysis buffer for 02:00:00 at 4 °C with gentle agitation.
Wash the beads two times with 10 volumes of lysis buffer and maintain them at 4 °C .
Dilute 20 µL of lysate of Rosetta (DE3) overexpressing rVapD with lysis buffer to a final volume of 100 µL .
Incubate the antibody-coupled beads with the bacterial lysate for 02:00:00 at 4 °C with gentle agitation.
Collect the immunoprecipitates by centrifugation at 500 x g, 4 °C, .
Elute the proteins complexes by adding 20 µL of 4× BMR buffer and boiling for 00:03:00 .
Incubate at 37 °C, Overnight .
Visualize the gel on an UV transilluminator.
Add 0.1 millimolar (mM) final concentration of IPTG.
Incubate the culture for 04:00:00 at 37 °C with shaking.
Wash the column with five column volumes of wash buffer.
Separate the sample by SDS-PAGE.
Stain the gel with Coomassie brilliant blue solution.
Incubate Overnight at 4 °C .
Block the unoccupied sites with 200 µL, /well of 5% skimmed milk diluted in PBT.
Incubate for 00:40:00 at 37 °C .
Wash three times with 200 µL, /well of PBT.
Wash three times with 200 µL, /well of PBT.
Incubate for 00:20:00 at Room temperature .
Read at 492nm in a spectrophotometer.
Incubate with the mouse serum (anti-VapD) at dilution of 1:500, 1:1,000, and 1:2,000 for 02:00:00 with gentle shaking at Room temperature .
Incubate with the secondary antibody (anti-mouse antibody H+L coupled to horseradish peroxidase) at a 1:2,000 dilution for 02:00:00 with gentle shaking at Room temperature .
Wash three times with PBT for 00:10:00 .

Perform the protein detection using a chemiluminescence reagent and a blot scanner. If a blot scanner is unavailable, the chemiluminescence signal can be detected in a dark room using radiographic plaques.
Incubate at 37 °C in humidified atmosphere, in the presence of 5% CO 2 .
Incubate at 37 °C Overnight in humidified atmosphere, in the presence of 5% CO 2 .
Incubate at 37 °C for 48:00:00 in humidified atmosphere, in the presence of 8% CO 2 .
Incubate at 37 °C for 03:00:00 in humidified atmosphere, in the presence of 5% CO 2 .
Incubate for an additional 02:00:00 .
Add 2.5 mL of DMEM supplemented with 2% fetal bovine serum.
Fix with fixative solution for 00:20:00 at Room temperature .
Incubate for 02:00:00 at Room temperature with anti-mouse Alexa Fluor 594 and anti-rabbit Alexa Fluor 488 antibodies diluted 1:100 in PBS 1×.
Mount with mounting medium for fluorescence (Antifade Mounting Medium).
Wash the cells with PBS 1X.
Incubate with DAPI (4´, 6-diamino-2-phenilindol) for 00:01:00 at Room temperature .
Resuspend the washed beads in five volumes of lysis buffer.
Centrifuge at 500 x g, , 1 minute .
Recover the beads by centrifugation at 500 x g, 4 °C, 1 minute , and discarding the supernatant (unbound antibody).
Wash three times with 100 µL of lysis buffer.
Load the samples on 20% SDS-PAGE gels and run.
Stain the gel with Coomassie brilliant blue solution.
Incubate the cells with mouse serum (anti- vapD) at a dilution of 1:100 in PBS 1× and anti- H. pylori at a dilution of 1:200, Overnight at 4 °C .

Spread 50 µL of the cells on an LB agar plate containing ampicillin ( 100 µg, /mL ). If no colonies appear after transformation, centrifuge the bacterial culture at 500 xg, discard the supernatant, and add 100 µl of fresh medium. Resuspend the cell pellet and spread thoroughly it onto the plate.
Add 0.7% sarkosyl, 3% Triton X-100, and 20 millimolar (mM) CHAPS, then mix gently for 00:15:00 at 4 °C using an end-over-end rotator.
creator: Alejandro Flores-Alanis
